description_long: This is a tall, rectangular stone obelisk with a slightly tapered top and a flat, truncated tip. The surface is adorned with multiple horizontal bands of inscriptions and relief carvings, some of which depict figures and symbols reminiscent of ancient civilizations. The stone appears weathered, with visible cracks and a rough, matte finish, suggesting significant age and exposure to the elements. The inscriptions and reliefs are most prominent on the front and side faces, while the rear face is more worn and less detailed. The base is simple and unadorned, blending seamlessly with the shaft, and there are no visible steps or platforms. The overall color is a dark gray with lighter streaks and patches, giving it a natural stone appearance.
description: A tall, dark gray stone obelisk with weathered inscriptions and relief carvings, featuring a flat, truncated tip and a simple, unadorned base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the most detailed inscriptions and relief carvings, with several horizontal bands and visible figures.
description_view_1: This side view reveals additional inscriptions and reliefs, though they are less prominent than the front, and the surface appears slightly more eroded.
description_view_2: The opposite side displays a mix of inscriptions and reliefs, with some areas more worn and less legible than the front.
description_view_3: The rear view is the most weathered, with faint or nearly illegible inscriptions and a generally darker, rougher surface.
attribute_inscription: Multiple horizontal bands of inscriptions and relief carvings are visible on the front and side faces, featuring ancient script and symbolic figures. The inscriptions are deeply engraved but show signs of weathering, making some sections less legible.
attribute_ornamentation: Relief carvings and symbolic motifs are present within the inscription bands, primarily on the front and sides. The style is ancient and geometric, with figures and patterns integrated into the stone.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple extension of the shaft, rectangular in shape, with no visible decorative features, steps, or platforms. It is made of the same stone material as the rest of the obelisk.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and truncated, lacking any decorative elements or pointed features. The finish is rough and consistent with the rest of the obelisk.
